<!-- Generated by spec-lite | skill: feature | date: 2026-06-09 -->

# Feature: HTTP File Transformer — Full `.http` Syntax Support & Unique Request Naming

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-002
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Users importing `.http`/`.rest` files (the ASP.NET Core / VS Code REST Client format) currently lose requests or get mangled results when files use `//` comments, `# @name` directives, file variables (`@var=value`), comments between headers, method-less request lines, or URL continuation lines — and duplicate request names are produced freely. This feature rewrites the parsing core of `HttpFileTransformer` ([HttpFileTransformer.ts](../../packages/core/src/utils/transformers/HttpFileTransformer.ts)) to support the full [Microsoft `.http` syntax](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/test/http-files?view=aspnetcore-10.0#http-file-syntax) and to guarantee unique, meaningful request names, so any real-world `.http` file imports faithfully.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No persisted-shape changes. The transformer's output is a Wave `Collection` conforming to the FEAT-001 schema (`info.version` stamped from `CURRENT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION`). Internal parse representation (module-private, in `HttpFileTransformer.ts`):

### Entities & Attributes

- **ParsedHttpRequest** (internal, replaces the current interface):
  - `name` (string) — final unique display name (set by the naming pass, not during block parse)
  - `method` (string) — uppercased; `'GET'` when the request line omits the method
  - `url` (string) — raw URL with `{{var}}` references preserved verbatim; continuation lines (`?`/`&`) already appended
  - `headers` (HeaderRow[]) — `Key: Value` pairs; interleaved comment lines skipped
  - `body` (string, optional) — everything after the first blank line following headers, trailing whitespace trimmed, comments NOT stripped
- **RequestBlock** (internal, new): the pre-parse unit between `###` separators:
  - `separatorText` (string, optional) — text after `###` (candidate name, priority 2)
  - `nameDirective` (string, optional) — value from `# @name x` / `// @name x` / `@name = x` variants (priority 1)
  - `leadingComment` (string, optional) — nearest non-directive comment above the request line (priority 2 fallback)
  - `lines` (string[]) — remaining content lines

### Relationships

- One `.http` file → N RequestBlocks → N ParsedHttpRequests → N `CollectionItem`s (flat, no folders), each with `item.name === item.request.name` (plan invariant).

### Indexes & Constraints

- **Name uniqueness**: a used-name set over the whole file; collisions get ` 2`, ` 3`, … suffixes. Applied once, after all blocks parse, to both `CollectionItem.name` and `request.name`.
- **File variables** (`@var=value` lines outside requests): recognized and skipped without error (no resolution — out of scope per plan; `{{var}}` references pass through verbatim).

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/utils/transformers/HttpFileTransformer.ts` — rewrite `parseHttpFile`/`parseRequestBlock` (split into block-splitter, request-line parser, header/body parser, naming pass); update `validate`/`canHandle`
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/transformers/HttpFileTransformer.test.ts` — table-driven unit tests with inline fixtures (extend existing file)
- `docs/features/collections.md` — document supported `.http` syntax in the import-formats section

## 4. Dependencies

- **FEAT-001** (Wave Schema Versioning): stamps `info.version` from `CURRENT_COLLECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION`. If FEAT-001 has not landed yet, keep `this.waveVersion` and leave a `// TODO(FEAT-001)` — the constant swap is part of FEAT-001's TASK-003 anyway.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Block splitter — separators, comments, file variables

- [ ] **Implementation**: Replace the `split(/(?:^|\n)(?:###|---)\s*/)` approach in `parseHttpFile` with a line-walking splitter that produces `RequestBlock`s:
  - Only lines starting with `###` delimit requests (drop `---` — not in the spec); capture trailing text as `separatorText`.
  - Outside the body, recognize `#`- and `//`-prefixed lines as comments; extract `@name` directives (regex tolerant of `# @name foo`, `// @name foo`, `#@name foo`, `@name = foo` spacing) into `nameDirective`, keep the nearest other comment as `leadingComment`, discard the rest.
  - Recognize file-variable lines (`/^@[\w-]+\s*=/`) and skip them silently.
  - Tolerate blank lines and arbitrary whitespace everywhere outside the body; content before the first `###` is a valid first block.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: file with no separators (single request); leading content before first `###`; `---` no longer splits (becomes body/ignored content as appropriate); `//` and `#` comments between requests ignored; `@name` directive captured in all spacing variants; `@var=value` lines skipped without error; empty blocks (separator followed by separator) produce no request.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Update the class-level JSDoc example to show `###`, `//` comments, `@name`, and file variables.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core test -- HttpFileTransformer` green; existing import of a simple two-request file still yields two requests.

### TASK-002: Request-line parsing — optional method, full method set, URL continuation

- [ ] **Implementation**: In the per-block parser:
  - Request line `[METHOD] URL [HTTP-version]`: method optional → default `GET`; accept `GET|POST|PUT|PATCH|DELETE|HEAD|OPTIONS|TRACE|CONNECT` (case-insensitive); trailing `HTTP/x.y` token ignored. A line that is a bare URL (starts with `http://`, `https://`, or `{{`) is a method-less request line.
  - After the request line, lines starting with `?` or `&` (before headers) are URL continuation lines — append (trimmed) to the URL per spec.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: method-less request defaults to GET; `TRACE`/`CONNECT` accepted; `GET https://x HTTP/1.1` ignores version token; multi-line query continuation (`?a=1` then `&b=2`) concatenates onto the URL; URL beginning with `{{baseUrl}}` parses; lowercase method normalizes to uppercase.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the request-line parser listing the accepted grammar.
- **Verify**: unit tests green; URL with continuation lines round-trips into `CollectionUrl.raw` intact.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-003: Headers & body parsing — interleaved comments, body fidelity

- [ ] **Implementation**: Headers are `Key: Value` lines after the request line until the first blank line; comment lines (`#`/`//`) interleaved between headers are skipped (today they break parsing); the obsolete `HTTP/` key-skip stays. Body is everything after that first blank line up to the block end: comments NOT stripped inside the body, internal blank lines preserved, only trailing whitespace trimmed. Body-to-`CollectionBody` mapping (raw + language detection from Content-Type) is unchanged.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: comment between two headers — both headers parsed; comment line inside a JSON body containing `#`/`//` preserved byte-for-byte; body with blank lines preserved; header value containing `://` parsed correctly (split on first colon); no body → `body` undefined; urlencoded/form content types still map via `detectBodyMode`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Inline comment documenting the "comments stop at the body boundary" rule (it's the spec's behavior, not an implementation convenience).
- **Verify**: unit tests green, including a fixture mirroring the plan's matrix row "body with `#` characters".
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-004: Naming strategy + uniqueness pass

- [ ] **Implementation**: After all blocks parse, resolve each request's name by priority: (1) `nameDirective`; (2) `separatorText` or `leadingComment` (nearest non-directive comment above the request); (3) URL-derived fallback — strip protocol and `{{var}}` segments, split remaining alphanumeric runs into words, produce a simple title (e.g., `GET api.example.com/users` → `Get users`-style). Then run the uniqueness pass: used-name set (exact match), collisions get ` 2`, ` 3`, …; assign the final name to **both** `CollectionItem.name` and `request.name` in `createCollectionItem` (plan invariant `item.name === request.name`).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: `@name` wins over separator text; separator text wins over URL fallback; URL fallback strips protocol and variables; three identical `@name dup` requests become `dup`, `dup 2`, `dup 3`; mixed-source collisions (directive vs. separator producing the same string) dedup; `item.name === item.request.name` asserted for every produced item.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on the naming resolver describing the three-tier priority and suffix scheme.
- **Verify**: importing a file with three unnamed requests to the same URL produces three distinctly named items; full transformer test suite green.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-005: Detection (`validate`/`canHandle`) update + docs

- [ ] **Implementation**: `validate`/`canHandle` currently require an uppercase-method line — with method-less requests now legal, broaden detection: content is `.http` if it contains a request line per TASK-002's grammar (method line **or** bare-URL line) or a `###` separator followed by one. Keep it conservative (FEAT-005's content detection will call `canHandle` on arbitrary text — garbage and JSON must still return `false`; `typeof data === 'string'` guard stays).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: method-less single-request file detected; JSON text rejected; YAML/OpenAPI text rejected; empty string rejected; plain prose rejected; `###`-separated file with bare URLs detected.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: `docs/features/collections.md` — replace/extend the `.http` import bullet with the supported syntax list (separators, comments, `@name`, file variables skipped, method-less requests, continuation lines, naming/dedup rules) and the explicit limitation that `{{var}}` references and `@var` values are not resolved (tracked in TODO).
- **Verify**: detection tests green; docs section renders and matches implemented behavior.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: `transformFrom` keeps its `Result<Collection, string>` contract — parse problems with individual blocks skip the block (best-effort import, current behavior); a file yielding zero requests returns `err('No valid HTTP requests found in file')`. Imported file content is untrusted input — the parser must never throw on arbitrary text (the existing outer `try/catch` stays as a last resort).
- **Logging**: N/A (pure function in core; errors surface through the import flow's existing notification path).

## 7. State Tracking

- [x] TASK-001: Block splitter — separators, comments, file variables
- [x] TASK-002: Request-line parsing — optional method, full method set, URL continuation
- [x] TASK-003: Headers & body parsing — interleaved comments, body fidelity
- [x] TASK-004: Naming strategy + uniqueness pass
- [x] TASK-005: Detection (`validate`/`canHandle`) update + docs

> **Implementation notes (2026-06-10)**: Implemented as a two-stage parser (`splitBlocks` → `parseRequestBlock` per block → `assignNames` pass) in `HttpFileTransformer.ts`; all 27 pre-existing tests pass unchanged, plus 35 new syntax-matrix tests in `HttpFileTransformer.syntax.test.ts`. Detection requires the method's target to look URL-ish (contains `://`, starts `{{` or `/`) so prose like "GET up early" is rejected.
